serviceNames.serviceName: Ride to a medical appointment
serviceNames.flavor: Wait time included — Dr. Pawtucket always runs late.

serviceNames.serviceName: Ride to physical therapy
Ride to dialysis (three times weekly)
serviceNames.flavor: Recurring — please coordinate a regular driver.

serviceNames.serviceName: Ride to the grocery store
Ride to the pharmacy
Ride to Sunday Mass at Saint Mary's
Ride to Twin River
serviceNames.flavor: Hard $20 limit, please enforce. She means it.

serviceNames.serviceName: Ride to the package store before the storm
serviceNames.flavor: French toast supplies optional; nips are not.

serviceNames.serviceName: Ride to the eye doctor (no driving home after dilation)
serviceNames.flavor: Member cannot drive themselves back.

serviceNames.serviceName: Ride to the RMV in Cranston
serviceNames.flavor: Bring a book. Bring two books.

serviceNames.serviceName: Ride to the airport (T.F. Green)
Ride to chemo and back
serviceNames.flavor: Gentle driver requested; bring crackers.

serviceNames.serviceName: Grocery run and unload
serviceNames.flavor: Don't forget the coffee milk.

serviceNames.serviceName: Pharmacy pickup
Pick up a coffee cabinet and a dozen Allie's donuts
serviceNames.flavor: It's a cabinet, not a milkshake. Cumberland location.

serviceNames.serviceName: Dunkin' run — large regular, extra extra
serviceNames.flavor: There's a Dunkin' on the way. There's always a Dunkin' on the way.

serviceNames.serviceName: Pick up a half-dozen stuffies and some chowdah
serviceNames.flavor: Red chowder is not chowder. Do not bring red chowder.

serviceNames.serviceName: Return library books to Cranston Public
Drop off mail at the post office
Gutter cleaning
serviceNames.flavor: Maple seeds again. Always the maple seeds.

serviceNames.serviceName: Change smoke-detector batteries
serviceNames.flavor: The chirping is driving everyone up the wall.

serviceNames.serviceName: Put up storm windows
serviceNames.flavor: Before the first nor'easter, ideally.

serviceNames.serviceName: Take down storm windows
serviceNames.flavor: Spring has sprung.

serviceNames.serviceName: Snow shoveling — walk and front steps
serviceNames.flavor: Salt is in the bucket by the door.

serviceNames.serviceName: Snowblow the driveway
serviceNames.flavor: Gas is in the red can. Pull twice and pray.

serviceNames.serviceName: Leaf raking and bagging
serviceNames.flavor: Town pickup is Thursday — use the paper bags.

serviceNames.serviceName: Change hard-to-reach light bulbs
serviceNames.flavor: The stairwell one needs the tall ladder.

serviceNames.serviceName: Move the AC units in / out for the season
serviceNames.flavor: Bedroom window, two of them, they're heavy.

serviceNames.serviceName: Turn the mattress and swap winter bedding
Bring trash and recycling to the curb
serviceNames.flavor: Recycling is every other week — check the magnet.

serviceNames.serviceName: Reach things on high shelves
Tighten the wobbly handrail on the back steps
Quahog rake needs restringing
serviceNames.flavor: He still goes clamming at 84. Respect.

serviceNames.serviceName: Open up the camp at Scarborough for the summer
serviceNames.flavor: Turn the water on, sweep out the field mice.

serviceNames.serviceName: Stack the firewood by the back door
Help programming the VCR
serviceNames.flavor: Clock still blinking 12:00 since the Clinton administration.

serviceNames.serviceName: Set up the new smartphone — again
serviceNames.flavor: She got a new one. We are all very excited.

serviceNames.serviceName: Reset the Wi-Fi router
serviceNames.flavor: It's the box with the blinking lights, by the window.

serviceNames.serviceName: Connect the new TV and find the Red Sox game
serviceNames.flavor: NESN, channel — well, that's the whole problem.

serviceNames.serviceName: Show me how to video call the grandkids
serviceNames.flavor: They're in California. It's very far.

serviceNames.serviceName: Untangle the email password situation
serviceNames.flavor: Written on a sticky note. Somewhere.

serviceNames.serviceName: Set up the medical-alert pendant
Help with the printer (it says PC LOAD LETTER)
Pair the new hearing aids to the phone
Fill out Medicare Part D paperwork
serviceNames.flavor: Open enrollment makes everyone want to lie down.

serviceNames.serviceName: Organize the weekly pill box
serviceNames.flavor: Seven days, two columns, do not improvise.

serviceNames.serviceName: Sit in on the cardiology appointment and take notes
serviceNames.flavor: An extra set of ears for the doctor's instructions.

serviceNames.serviceName: Pick up a prescription and explain the new dosage
Help reading and sorting the mail
serviceNames.flavor: Especially anything that says URGENT in red.

serviceNames.serviceName: Help paying bills online
Sort out a confusing hospital bill
serviceNames.flavor: It says $0.00 and also $4,200. Both, somehow.

serviceNames.serviceName: Fill out the rent-rebate / property-tax form
Shred a box of old statements
Notarize a couple of documents
Friendly phone call, twice a week
serviceNames.flavor: Mostly to talk about the Sox and the weather.

serviceNames.serviceName: Weekly visit for a cup of coffee and a chat
serviceNames.flavor: Bring your own mug; hers are for company.

serviceNames.serviceName: Take the dog (Brady) for a walk
serviceNames.flavor: Named after Tom. Don't mention the move to Tampa.

serviceNames.serviceName: Walk to the bubbler and back for fresh air
serviceNames.flavor: A little loop around the block does wonders.

serviceNames.serviceName: Play cards — she cheats at cribbage, fair warning
serviceNames.flavor: Fifteen-two, fifteen-four, and a hand you'll never see.

serviceNames.serviceName: Read the ProJo aloud over tea
serviceNames.flavor: Obituaries first — it's a Rhode Island thing.

serviceNames.serviceName: Drive a new member to a welcome coffee
Home visit to complete the intake questionnaire
Walk a new member through the member handbook
Help connect to Meals on Wheels
Refer to the Point / RI fuel-assistance program
serviceNames.flavor: Heating oil isn't getting any cheaper.

serviceNames.serviceName: Connect with the RIde paratransit application
Refer to a vetted handyman for the big repairs
serviceNames.flavor: Beyond what our volunteers can safely do.

serviceNames.serviceName: Welfare check — no answer on the phone since yesterday
serviceNames.flavor: Please call the office if anything seems off.

serviceNames.serviceName: Storm check-in — power's out on the East Side
serviceNames.flavor: Make sure she has water, a flashlight, and company.

serviceNames.serviceName: Heat-wave check during the July scorcher
serviceNames.flavor: No AC upstairs — bring a fan if you have a spare.

serviceNames.serviceName: Bring a meal during a rough recovery week
Coordinate the spring volunteer meeting
serviceNames.flavor: Coffee, an agenda, and someone to take minutes.

serviceNames.serviceName: Help draft the quarterly member newsletter
Renew the village's affiliation paperwork
serviceNames.flavor: The annual Village-to-Village Network dues.

serviceNames.serviceName: Represent our village at the statewide gathering
serviceNames.flavor: It's in Warwick this year. There will be a buffet.

serviceNames.serviceName: Coordinate a shared volunteer day with a neighboring village
Organize a circle of three for a homebound member
serviceNames.flavor: Rotating drivers so no one volunteer burns out.

serviceNames.serviceName: Set up a meal-train circle after surgery
serviceNames.flavor: Sign-up sheet on the fridge; label what's in the freezer.

serviceNames.serviceName: Coordinate a caregiver-respite circle
serviceNames.flavor: Give the daughter a Saturday off, just one.

transportationTypes.flavor: The backbone of the whole operation.
Senior fare with the bus pass.
Book it 48 hours ahead or forget it.
For the short hops around the neighborhood.
For appointments outside our coverage area.

householdChores.chore: Swapping AC units for storm windows (and back)
Spring yard cleanup and bed prep
Watering plants during a heat wave
Changing smoke and CO detector batteries
Bringing trash and recycling to the curb
Minor repairs — wobbly handrails, sticky doors

contactTypes: Group activity at the senior center
Card or letter in the mail
activityTypes: Played cards or a board game
Watched the Red Sox / Patriots game together
Watered the plants / small garden help
Read the newspaper / a book aloud
Helped write a letter or card
Short walk to the mailbox for fresh air
Just listened — they needed to talk
sampleNotes: Good visit. We split a coffee cabinet and solved the whole Red Sox bullpen in forty-five minutes.
Phone seemed a little down today — daughter's flight got cancelled. Stayed on the line an extra twenty.
Fixed the TV (input was on HDMI 3 again) and we caught the last two innings. He was thrilled.
Brought the ProJo and read the obituaries — 'checking I'm not in there,' she says every time.
Walked Brady around the block twice. Both of them slept well after, I'd bet.
Helped sort a scary-looking hospital bill. Turned out to be a duplicate — no charge. Big relief.
She beat me at cribbage. I still don't know how. Skunked, honestly.
Porch visit, power was out from the storm. Has water, a flashlight, and a fully charged spirit.
Practiced the FaceTime button about six times. We got the grandkids on the line — happy tears.
Quiet visit. He just wanted company since Marie passed. Mostly we sat and watched the birds.

memberDropReasons: Health declined beyond what the village can support
Financial hardship — unable to continue membership
Unable to reach after repeated attempts
Requested to pause / take a break
